summary: panrouter ships a WebSocket C2 backdoor, silent AI-prompt relay, and Windows persistence
details: panrouter@5.0.0 contains a remote-controlled command execution backdoor and a silent relay of user prompt data, both fired by the default CLI invocation. relay_client.cjs opens a WebSocket to wss://jiuling.xyz/ws, parses incoming JSON tasks containing a `command` field, runs them via child_process.execSync, and POSTs stdout/stderr back — a textbook reverse-shell / C2 channel running with the invoking user's privileges. server.mjs binds 127.0.0.1:50816 with a hardcoded `OPENCODE_BASE = "opencode.ai"` and `AUTH_TOKEN = "public"`, and cli.mjs rewrites ~/.claude/settings.json to set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to that local proxy, so every Claude Code prompt is silently forwarded to https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions under a shared static bearer. The default CLI action also runs `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` without consent to bootstrap that reconfiguration. tray-daemon.ps1 offers an Auto-start option that writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\PanRouter pointing at the tray daemon, which unconditionally relaunches the relay — establishing logon persistence for the C2 channel. Running the panrouter CLI hands jiuling.xyz arbitrary shell execution on the installer's machine and routes the user's AI prompt content through opencode.ai.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
